title: Leek & Potato Soup

overview.description: A classic British soup made with gently softened leeks and floury potatoes, blended until smooth. Simple, comforting and perfect with fresh bread or a toasted sandwich.

method: Melt the butter in a large saucepan over a low to medium heat.
Add the leeks and onion and cook gently for about 10–15 minutes until completely softened but not browned.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the potatoes and pour in the stock.
Bring to the boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer for 20 minutes until the potatoes are very tender.
Blend until completely smooth using a stick blender or countertop blender.
If using, stir in the double cream and heat through gently without boiling.
Season to taste with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
Serve topped with chopped chives if desired.

learning.did-you-know: Leeks have been grown in Britain for centuries and are one of the country's most traditional soup vegetables.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How gently sweating vegetables develops sweetness without caramelisation, why floury potatoes create a naturally creamy texture, and when to add cream without splitting it.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A dependable family favourite that's inexpensive, easy to make after work and freezes exceptionally well. It's an ideal introduction to homemade soups.
chapman-tips: Wash the sliced leeks thoroughly as they often hide grit between the layers.
Maris Piper or King Edward potatoes produce the smoothest texture.
For a lighter soup, simply leave out the cream.
notes.batch-cooking: Double the recipe and freeze in family-sized portions for up to three months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently, adding a splash of stock if the soup has thickened during storage.
